summary: Russian forces report ongoing FPV drone operations in Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman, with the Rubikon Center claiming 110 enemy personnel eliminated on March 18, including anti-drone missions against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs and EW systems. Ukrainian operators are adapting tactics, using log-periodic directional antennas on Molniyas to extend range and resist jamming, while also experimenting with pre-strike maneuvers like attempting to dismount Russian troops before engaging. In Iraq, Iranian-backed militias launched a fiber-optic FPV drone attack on the US Victory Base near Baghdad. On the ground, Ukrainian drones have struck Russian infantry, including a soldier hit by two simultaneous FPVs while hiding behind a tree, and a Russian infantryman who charged a drone with a bayonet and was killed.
